algorithms.title: Neurological Emergency Assessment Sequence
algorithms.description: A structured approach to altered neurological presentations, including stroke and elevated ICP recognition.
steps: Check blood glucose immediately in any stroke-like presentation to rule out a hypoglycemic/hyperglycemic mimic.
Apply FAST: assess Face, Arm, and Speech findings, and establish an exact last known well Time.
Assess vital signs for Cushing's Triad (hypertension, bradycardia, altered respiratory pattern) suggesting elevated intracranial pressure.
In a headache patient, perform a full neurological exam; escalate concern for red flags (worst-ever headache, focal deficit, fever with neck stiffness).
If fever, nuchal rigidity, and altered mental status are present together, suspect meningitis and apply appropriate precautions.
Transport promptly with reassessment en route, given the time-sensitive nature of stroke and elevated-ICP emergencies.
